records.display_name: Brooklyn Bus Network Redesign Color-Coded Route Types

payload.claim_text: The Draft Plan proposes five color-coded route types: green for Local routes, purple for Rushroutes, red for Limited routes, blue for Crosstown (SBS) routes, and dark green for Express routes.
payload.subject: Brooklyn Bus Network Redesign Draft Plan

records.display_name: Brooklyn Population Growth Since 2010

payload.claim_text: The borough's population has grown 5.2% since 2010 and according to Census data, and about 55% of Brooklyn households do not own a vehicle.
payload.subject: Brooklyn demographics

records.display_name: Bus Open Stroller Pilot Started October 2022

payload.claim_text: In October we started our Bus Open Stroller Pilot, allowing parents and caregivers to bring an open, unfolded stroller on seven select NYCT and MTA bus routes.
payload.subject: Bus Open Stroller Pilot

records.display_name: Zoning for Accessibility First Year Implementation

payload.claim_text: This was also the first year of implementation for Zoning for Accessibility, which is already yielding dividends with private developers committing to adding elevators at stations in Manhattan and Queens.
payload.subject: Zoning for Accessibility

records.display_name: NYCT Faster, Cleaner, Safer Plan

payload.claim_text: As part of our NYCT Faster, Cleaner, Safer Plan, this month the Department of Subways will deliver a presentation on the Year-End results yielded from the Speed Unit established in October 2021.
payload.subject: NYCT Faster Cleaner Safer Plan

records.display_name: Speed Unit Established October 2021

payload.claim_text: The Department of Subways will deliver a presentation on the Year-End results yielded from the Speed Unit established in October 2021. This group works with all departments at NYCT to increase train speeds, reduce delays, and improve operating efficiency.
payload.subject: Department of Subways Speed Unit

records.display_name: ADA Settlement Agreement for Accessibility by 2055

payload.claim_text: This year we announced a settlement agreement with disability and transit advocates that provides a clear path toward accessibility across the subway system by 2055 — putting a timeline to this commitment for the first time.
payload.subject: subway accessibility

records.display_name: Subway Lines with Over 90% On-Time Performance

payload.claim_text: In November, the 7, G, L, and all three shuttles had over 90% on time performance.
payload.subject: subway line performance

records.display_name: Station Agent Hiring Numbers

payload.claim_text: 288 new station agents have been hired since November 2021 and we anticipate hiring an additional 235 through the first half of 2023.
payload.subject: station agent hiring

records.display_name: Brooklyn Bus Network Redesign Public Workshops

payload.description: 18 public workshops to gather input on Brooklyn Bus Network Redesign Draft Plan from customers and Brooklyn residents.

records.display_name: 7th TransitTalk at Utica Avenue A/C Station

payload.description: 7th TransitTalk event at Utica Avenue A/C station in Brooklyn, providing one-on-one engagement with customers at their neighborhood station.

records.display_name: Station Agents New Role Phased In

payload.description: Department of Subways will begin to phase in the new station agent role in early 2023, with training underway on enhanced customer service and OMNY equipment.

records.display_name: Customer Service Centers opening in early 2023

payload.description: Customer Service Centers will open in six subway stations in early 2023, with nine additional stations expected later in the year.

records.display_name: Reduced Fare OMNY Event at Bay Ridge Senior Center

payload.description: MTA Government and Community Relations and Systemwide Accessibility teams held event at Bay Ridge Center in Bay Ridge, Brooklyn to discuss OMNY rollout and Reduced Fare options, joined by NYS Senator Andrew Gounardes.

records.display_name: New Post-COVID Subway Ridership High

payload.description: New post-COVID high of 3.93 million subway riders on December 8th, likely due to shopping and tourist travel for holidays.

records.display_name: Public Solicitation of Bids for 470 Battery-Electric Buses

payload.description: Public solicitation of bids for 470 battery-electric buses (380 standard and 90 articulated), projected to be deployed at 11 depots. First tranche projected in 2025, rest in 2026.

records.display_name: Bus Lane Enforcement Cameras Activated on Final Three Routes

payload.description: Final three bus lane enforcement camera routes (B25, B62, B42) activated, extending onboard camera coverage to 50% of existing bus lanes.

records.display_name: Station Agents Customer Service Improvements

payload.description: Historic agreement with TWU Local 100 to modernize the station agent role, bringing agents out of booths to perform core customer service functions at turnstiles, fare machines, and throughout stations. Training underway on enhanced customer service and OMNY equipment.

records.display_name: Customer Service Centers in Subway Stations

payload.description: Comprehensive Customer Service Centers in the subway system providing direct customer support with OMNY use and Reduced-Fare support. Will open in six subway stations in early 2023, with nine additional stations expected later in the year.

records.display_name: All-Electric Bus Fleet Transition

payload.description: Commitment to transition to a fully all-electric fleet by 2040. 2020-2024 Capital Program includes funding for up to 500 electric buses and charging infrastructure. Public solicitation of bids for 470 battery-electric buses (380 standard and 90 articulated). Buses projected to be deployed at 11 depots, delivered in two tranches: first projected in 2025, rest in 2026.

records.display_name: Brooklyn Bus Network Redesign

description: Currently in planning stages. Draft plan contains proposal for new SBS route between Kensington, Brooklyn and JFK Airport (B55 SBS). Final plan expected in second half of 2025.
Draft Plan for the Brooklyn Bus Network Redesign, proposing a reimagined Brooklyn bus network with routes, stops, and generalized frequencies.

Comprehensive redesign of the Brooklyn bus network with proposed routes, stops, and generalized frequencies reimagined to meet the needs of current and future customers. The Draft Plan was published December 1, 2022, proposing 69 local bus routes (54 Local, 3 Rush, 7 Limited, 5 Crosstown/SBS) plus express routes.

Release of the Proposed Final Plan expected in late 2025. Addresses concerns of riders and other stakeholders received in Draft Plan feedback. Public will have another opportunity to give feedback through additional round of outreach.

The MTA released a draft plan for the Brooklyn Bus Network Redesign – the first overhaul of Brooklyn bus service in decades. The proposals create a simpler bus network to encourage bus ridership, reduce travel time and strengthen interborough bus travel. The plan proposes 69 local routes and 19 express routes, for a total of 88 routes.
Aims to create a simpler bus network to encourage bus ridership, reduce travel time and strengthen interborough bus travel
Draft Brooklyn Bus Network Redesign Plan released last month (December 2022), the first overhaul of Brooklyn bus service in decades
A draft Brooklyn Bus Network Redesign includes changes to local and express buses will be released later this year
Outreach pop-up events and participation at NYTM BusFest 2023
Draft Plan published; 15 in-person public events held between April 14 and May 11, 2023; 18 virtual workshops held January-March 2023
OP is preparing the Proposed Final Plan based on the nearly 8,000 comments received in late 2022 and 2023

Part of NYCT's larger effort to modernize New York City's bus network and improve bus service borough by borough. The Draft Plan proposed 69 local routes and 19 express routes to better serve the borough's 650,000 weekday riders.
status: Draft Plan released in late 2022; Proposed Final Plan expected later this year (2024)

payload.description: Currently in planning stages. Draft plan contains proposal for new SBS route between Kensington, Brooklyn and JFK Airport (B55 SBS). Final plan expected in second half of 2025.

records.raw_text: The Brooklyn Bus Network Redesign is part of the MTA's larger effort to comprehensively modernize New York City's bus network and improve bus service borough by borough.

records.display_name: Fuel Management System Upgrade at Bus Depots

payload.description: Contract to Fleetwatch for $6.35M to upgrade fuel management systems at bus depots. Fleetwatch currently deployed at Grand Avenue and Mother Clara Hale depots. Contract will enable installation at all remaining bus depots over two years. System expected to last approximately 20 years.
payload.status: requesting Board approval to award contract

records.display_name: Bus Lane Enforcement Camera Expansion

payload.description: Expanded Bus lane enforcement cameras on 300 additional buses and 9 new routes. Final three routes activated on December 12th include B25, B62, and B42. Extends onboard camera coverage to 50% of existing bus lanes serving 265K (pre-pandemic) average weekday customers across all five boroughs. Goal to expand with 600 additional cameras by end of 2023, covering up to 85% of existing bus lanes.
